rationale: Multi-factor analysis covering stationarity, ACF/PACF interpretation, information criteria trade-offs (AICc vs BIC), diagnostic checks, and practical sample-size guidance with calibrated hedging, though lacks an explicit assumption ledger and does not ask for additional context.
rubric_evidence: BIC is often preferred if you want a more conservative choice, while AICc is good for predictive accuracy
Reject models whose residuals still show autocorrelation by checking the residual ACF and a Ljung–Box test
keep the search small—typically p, q ∈ {0,1,2,3}, and rarely above 4 unless there is very strong evidence
